content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Randy Risner 
Chief Assistant City Attorney 
555 Santa Clara Street 
Vallejo, CA 94590 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-228 
Dear Mr. Risner: 
December 12, 2019 
This letter responds to your request to reconsider our previous advice letter to 
Councilmember Robert McConnell, No. A-18-053, regarding Government code Section 1090 
("Section 1090") 1 in light of the recent amendments to the California Fair Employment and 
Housing Act effective in 2020 in Senate Bill 329, c. 600 of2019.2 
In the No. 18-053 advice letter, we advised Councilmember McConnell that he is prohibited 
from making Section 8 housing assistance contracts under Section 1090 where he is a public official 
on the Vallejo Housing Authority ("VHA") Board-and the landlord with a financial interest in the 
contract, unless his factual situation meets the remote interest exception in Section 1091(b)(12).3 
This remote interest exception permits such contracts only where (1) a Section 8 contract was in 
existence prior to the landlord becoming a public official and the contract will be renewed as to the 
same tenant, or (2) as to a new tenant if the public official is in a jurisdiction with a rental vacancy 
rate of less than 5 percent and the contract will be for a unit previously under a Section 8 contract. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 This legislation was double joined with two additional bills: AB 1497, c. 599 and SB 222, c. 601, of 2020. 
Senate Bill 222, \lS the last chaptered bill ordered the joined language and enacted amendments to Government Code 
section 12927 and 12955 proposed by all three bills. (See Sections 14 and 16, SB 222, c. 601 of2019.) 
. 3 Government Code section 1091(b)(l2) defines the remote interest as: 
(12) That of an elected officer otherwise subject to Section 1090, in any 
housing assistance payment contract entered into pursuant to Section 8 of the United 
States Housing Act of 1937 (42 U.S.C. Sec. 1437.f) as amended, provided that the 
housing assistance payment contract was in existence before Section l 090 became 
applicable to the officer and will be renewed or extended only as to the existing 
tenant, or, in a jurisdiction in which the rental vacancy rate is less than 5 percent, as 
to new tenants in a unit previously under a Section 8 contract. This-section applies to 
any person who became a public official on or after November 1; 1986. 


File No. A-19-228 
Page No. 2 
The recent California Fair Employment and Housing Act amendments expand the definition 
of "source of income" in order to prohibit landlords from discriminating against tenants who rely 
upon Section 8 housing assistance contracts for assistance in paying their rent.4 You request 
whether Councilmember McConnel can enter into a Section 8 housing contract without violating 
Section 1090 considering these changes. 
We have examined the recent legislation and find that there are no relevant changes to the 
law or the facts such that it is appropriate to reconsider our earlier advice. Councilmember 
McConnell is prohibited under Section 1090 from entering into a VHA contract unless the factual 
situation meets the definition of a remote interest under Section 1091 (b )( 12). 
We note the California Fair Employment and Housing Act prohibitions against 
discrimination do not prohibit a finding of a conflict of interest under Section 1090. 5 Further, the 
recent legislation did not alter or expand the remote interest exception in Section 1091(b)(12). 
Additionally, the Senate Floor Analysis for the legislat_ion recognizes that while Senate Bill 329 
would prohibit landlords from discriminating against tenants with Section 8 vouchers, landlords 
may reject tenancy applications on "otherwise lawful grounds that are not based on a participant's 
receipt of a housing subsidy."6 Under Section 1090, the determining factor in limiting your ability 
to enter into a Section 8 housing assistance contract in your official capacity and your private 
capacity and is whether you have a prohibited financial interest in the contract. Where a contract is 
prohibited under Section 1090, the contract is null and void. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
LKH:aja 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
~£:tj~ 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
4 Senate Floor Analysis of Senate Bill 329, dated September 10, 2019, pp. 1-2) 
5 65 Ops. Cal. Atty. Gen. 305 (1982); relating to discrimination in employment due to marital status 
concerning a public official and his spouse and the application of Section 1090. 
6 Senate Floor Analysis of Senate Bill 329, dated September 10, 2019, p: 4.

sections.question: Whether Councilmember McConnell can enter into a Section 8 housing contract without violating Section 1090 considering the recent amendments to the California Fair Employment and Housing Act effective in 2020 in Senate Bill 329, c. 600 of 2019.
sections.conclusion: We have examined the recent legislation and find that there are no relevant changes to the law or the facts such that it is appropriate to reconsider our earlier advice. Councilmember McConnell is prohibited under Section 1090 from entering into a VHA contract unless the factual situation meets the definition of a remote interest under Section 1091(b)(12). The California Fair Employment and Housing Act prohibitions against discrimination do not prohibit a finding of a conflict of interest under Section 1090. Further, the recent legislation did not alter or expand the remote interest exception in Section 1091(b)(12).
sections.question_synthetic: Does the 2020 amendment to the Fair Employment and Housing Act (Senate Bill 329) change whether a City Councilmember and Vallejo Housing Authority Board member can enter into Section 8 housing assistance contracts where he has a financial interest as a landlord?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to reconsider its prior advice that Councilmember McConnell is prohibited from entering into Section 8 housing contracts under Section 1090 because the recent Fair Employment and Housing Act amendments do not change the law or facts relevant to the conflict of interest analysis. The discrimination protections in the new legislation do not override Section 1090's financial interest prohibition, and the remote interest exception remains unchanged.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter with explicit question and conclusion sections. Document addresses reconsideration of prior advice letter A-18-053. References to Government Code sections and legislative analysis clearly establish legal framework.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilmember McConnell can enter into a Section 8 housing contract without violating Section 1090 considering the recent amendments to the California Fair Employment and Housing Act effective in 2020 in Senate Bill 329, c. 600 of 2019.

CONCLUSION: We have examined the recent legislation and find that there are no relevant changes to the law or the facts such that it is appropriate to reconsider our earlier advice. Councilmember McConnell is prohibited under Section 1090 from entering into a VHA contract unless the factual situation meets the definition of a remote interest under Section 1091(b)(12). The California Fair Employment and Housing Act prohibitions against discrimination do not prohibit a finding of a conflict of interest under Section 1090. Further, the recent legislation did not alter or expand the remote interest exception in Section 1091(b)(12).

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request to reconsider prior advice on whether recent amendments to the Fair Employment and Housing Act affect a Councilmember's ability to enter into Section 8 housing contracts while serving on a Housing Authority board. The FPPC affirms its prior position that such contracts remain prohibited under Section 1090 unless the remote interest exception applies, finding that the new legislation does not alter this conflict of interest analysis.
